business.headline: 41% borrowers are Gen Z: Is hidden debt rising among young Indians?A growing number of young Indians are turning to credit early in life. With Gen Z now making up 41% of borrowers, is this a sign of financial confidence or a warning of rising hidden debt? Let's find out.

LPG booking timelines unchanged; Centre assures steady supplyAuthorities have urged people not to believe or share unverified information. They have also advised against panic booking of cylinders, stressing that there is enough supply available across the country.

8th Pay Commission: Can government salaries finally match private sector perks?For years, government employees have measured their pay against private sector packages. Now, with the 8th Pay Commission seeking feedback, will it lead to a more balanced pay structure? Let's have a look.

Sensex jumps 1,500 points: Why is stock market rising today despite Iran war?Sensex, Nifty jump: The main trigger for today's rally is growing hope that tensions in the Iran conflict may ease. Reports suggest that the United States is working on a possible month-long ceasefire and has shared a 15-point plan with Iran.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets rise as crude oil prices fall?Early signals suggest that investors may return to equities after a volatile phase driven by war concerns and rising energy costs. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,095 as of 8:03 am, indicating that the Nifty may open above Tuesday's close of 22,912.40.
Sensex, Nifty gain over hopes of de-escalation in West Asia WarSensex jumps 700 points, Nifty above 23,100; SBI up 2%

West Asia war brings Indian aviation to its kneesA spike in global crude prices, closure of busy aviation hubs, and sweeping airspace restrictions across West Asia have collectively delivered a body blow to the airline industry that no carrier was prepared for.

Not just $580 million oil trades, huge S&P bets were placed just before Trump postWhat began with unusually timed oil trades worth around $580 million has now widened, with massive bets in S&P 500 futures also coming under scrutiny, even as the White House has dismissed the allegations.

Bangladesh hikes jet fuel prices by nearly 80% amid global energy crisisThe Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission (BERC) on Tuesday increased jet fuel prices by nearly 80 percent, marking the second revision this month. The move comes as the ripple effect of rising global fuel rates, as tensions in key oil-producing regions continue to disrupt market stability.
Reliance buys 5 million barrels of Iranian oil after US waiver, sources sayThe development comes after the US administration announced a 30-day waiver on certain sanctions related to Iranian oil.

India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand: RTIAn RTI reply has suggested that India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand, even as global supply risks rise amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

Only 40% women invest beyond FDs and gold: Why the gap still exists?Indian women are known for their strong saving habits, but when it comes to investing beyond fixed deposits and gold, many still hold back. What is stopping them from taking that next step?
Markets bounce back, Sensex up 1,300 points, banks leadThe Sensex surged 1,372 points to close at 74,068.45, while the Nifty 50 rose 399.75 points to settle at 22,912.40, reclaiming the 22,900 mark.

Markets in panic mode, gold and silver also hit: What's left for investors?Since the Iran war began, the Sensex has fallen about 10.6%, while the Nifty has dropped nearly 11%. At the same time, gold and silver have also declined, leaving investors with limited places to hide.

Form 16 gone from April 2026: What will your employer give you instead?A key document for salaried taxpayers is set to change. Starting April 2026, Form 16 may no longer be issued, with a new format stepping in to reshape how your income details are reported.

Goldman Sachs cuts India growth forecast to 5.9%, flags rate hike risksThe revision follows changes in Goldman's assumptions on oil prices and supply disruptions. Higher crude prices remain a major risk for India, which relies heavily on imports.

8th Pay Commission: When will your salary rise and how much can you getCentral government employees are eagerly waiting for the 8th Pay Commission update. While a decent salary hike is expected, the bigger question remains, i.e., when will the revised pay actually start coming in?

Swiggy hikes platform fee to Rs 17.58 after Zomato, food orders get costlierFood delivery bills are inching up again. Swiggy has increased its platform fee soon after Zomato, meaning customers will now pay more each time they order online.

Bought gold during Iran war? Here's why it's not giving you returnsGold and silver prices have fallen despite ongoing war tensions, which is unusual as precious metals are typically seen as a safe investment during crises like war.

Kotak Mahindra Bank shares rise: Why is the stock gaining today?The sharp rise reflects a recovery after recent weakness, but investors should watch for consistency in momentum as broader market trends and foreign investor flows continue to influence banking stocks.

entertainment.headline: Anurag Dobhal fears he may not walk again after car crash: Leg is numb Bigg Boss 17 fame Anurag Dobhal has shared a distressing health update after a severe car crash, revealing one of his legs is completely numb. He fears he may never walk again.

The most haunting scene in Dhurandhar 2 that says everything without saying much Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar 2 is high on testosterone - it hits more than it says. Yet, its most haunting, cathartic moment comes without any gore. Here's breaking down the scene with the potential of leaving you speechless, wondering and taking a leaf out of Jassi's emotional playbook.

Grey's Anatomy's Caterina Scorsone-Fightmaster spark dating buzz, walk hand-in-hand Grey's Anatomy co-stars Caterina Scorsone and ER Fightmaster have sparked dating rumours after being seen together in Los Angeles, hinting at an off-screen romance. They played each other's love interest in Season 19 of the show.

Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla declared family-friendly, gets UA 16 rating  Actor Akshay Kumar's horror-comedy Bhooth Bangla secured UA 16 certificate. The CBFC advised parental guidance for the Priyadarshan directorial, particularly for viewers under 16, owing to its spooky themes.

Punjab doctor who treated Arjun Rampal lands surprise role in Dhurandhar 2 A Punjab doctor who treated Arjun Rampal on the Dhurandhar: The Revenge set landed a surprise cameo alongside Ranveer Singh. Here's how it happened.

Dhurandhar to KGF Chapter 2: Sanjay Dutt scores 3 Rs 1,000 crore hits Sanjay Dutt has remarkably delivered three consecutive Rs 1,000 crore hits. His powerful performances in key supporting and antagonist roles continue to captivate audiences and command massive box office success.

Reacher's Alan Ritchson gets clean chit in neighbour brawl, acted in self-defence Reacher star Alan Ritchson will not face charges in a neighbour altercation after police ruled his actions were in self-defence. Officials confirmed the case is closed since Ritchson has also declined to press charges against his neighbour.

Pressing national issue: Pak actor Ali Zafar's jibe at critics over Eid outfit row Ali Zafar issued an "apology" for wearing a kurta without an undershirt on Eid after several social media users trolled him online. The actor assured that he would make "every effort to avoid repeating" such a mistake.

Nobody Wants This 3 goes on floors, Kristen Bell posts candid set photos The cast and crew of Nobody Wants This have begun filming season 3. Actor Kristen Bell shared behind-the-scenes photos from the set alongside Adam Brody and other co-stars.

Logan Lerman, Emily Bader in 13 going on 30 remake: Here's what we know Netflix confirmed a 13 Going on 30 remake starring Logan Lerman and Emily Bader. Jennifer Garner, who starred in the 2004 film, has come on board as executive producer for the new version.

Shalini Pandey wants action film with Ranveer, calls Dhurandhar a favourite Shalini Pandey said in an exclusive conversation that she wants to explore action films and named Ranveer Singh as a dream co-star. She also called Dhurandhar one of her recent favourite films.

Dhurandhar 2 box office worldwide Day 6: Rs 2,000 crore combined, smashes Kantara 2 The Dhurandhar series is now Rs 2,000 crore strong. It is also the only Indian franchise to have two Rs 500 crore films - all this when the latest instalment has only wrapped up its first Tuesday.

Mom-to-be Divyanka Tripathi walks into 'room full of love', shares baby shower pic Divyanka Tripathi beams at a surprise baby shower with Vivek Dahiya by her side, as the couple prepares for parenthood in June 2026.

Kim Kardashian, Lewis Hamilton walk arm-in-arm in Japan. Watch viral video Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton were seen together in Tokyo, fueling fresh speculation about their relationship. A viral video adds a new twist to the ongoing dating rumours, keeping fans and media abuzz.

Dhurandhar 2's Rizwan writes a note for Ranveer Singh: Will always have your back Dhurandhar 2 continues to dominate the box office, cementing its position as one of the year's biggest hits. Mustafa Ahmed, who plays Rizwan in the film, wrote an emotional note to Ranveer Singh, offering a deeper glimpse into the bond that shapes their on-screen partnership.

Even fake collections don't reach Rs 100 crore: Kunal Kohli on Dhurandhar's success Kunal Kohli praised the box office success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He expressed admiration for Ranveer Singh and Aditya Dhar for showing the true potential of Indian cinema.

R Madhavan on Dhurandhar 2 smoking scene row: No intention to hurt Sikh sentiments R Madhavan responded to the controversy around a Dhurandhar: The Revenge scene, clarifying that there was no disrespect intended towards the Dasam Granth. The actor called it a misunderstanding.

general news.headline: No LPG for homes that don't take up PNG where available: Govt's piped gas pushA looming gas crunch and a new rule could change how millions cook, with a strict deadline now in place, households may soon face a choice that is not really optional.

Biggest oil disruption in history? Energy hubs are up in flames in US, Russia tooOil and gas refineries and hubs are up in flames not just in the Middle East, but also in Russia and the US. Crude oil prices have surged over $100 a barrel. With the energy infrastructure in the Middle East likely to take years to be rebuilt, the world could be set for the biggest oil disruption in history.

Amid the Iran war, shipping has been squeezed to a trickle. How many ships are struck? Which countries have been allowed passage? Is Iran charging a toll? We have divided the article into sections. Read on to find out.

Remember Lalu's rebel son? Krishna bhakt-Teju Bhaiya is now a Shaivite santLalu Prasad Yadav's elder son, Tej Pratap, is speaking of wisdom and devotion again. He's no longer the Krishna bhakt that he was. He is now sporting saffron attire in Reels, and is propagating Shiva bhakti. His new Instagram handle is SantTejPratapYadav. After political and personal isolation, Teju bhaiya seems to have found solace in Rudraksha, Shiva bhajans and the Shiva Puran.

Live: Israel strikes 2 Iran sites producing sea cruise missilesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: On the fourth week of the US-Israel-Iran war, Washington sent a 15-point truce plan to Tehran to end the Middle East conflict. The conditions include strict curbs on Tehran's nuclear programme, an end to support for proxy groups and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Washington is also pushing for a month-long ceasefire to enable negotiations. However, with Iran's military dismissing Trump's claim of negotiation talks, it seems unlikely that Iran will accept a proposal compromising on its nuclear facilities. 

Meanwhile, both Iran and Israel continue to attack each other on Wednesday. Israel targeted several places in Tehran with newly launched air raids on Wednesday. Iran, on the other hand, targeted US and Israeli military bases in the Gulf region in retaliatory measures. Catch all the live updates of the prevailing Middle East conflict on Indiatoday.in-

Congress told to vacate Delhi's Akbar, Raisina Road offices by SaturdayCongress faces a deadline to vacate two of its most important Delhi addresses. With legal options on the table and time running out, the party now scrambles to hold on to its political nerve centre.

Actor-model dies in Jaipur accident, late-night crash caught on cameraActor-model Harshil Kalia died in Jaipur when her car rammed a divider and flipped onto its left side, causing severe head injuries. She was taken to a nearby hospital, where doctors declared her dead.

AIADMK's first list for Tamil Nadu polls out, EPS to contest from EdappadiTamil Nadu's main opposition party, AIADMK, released its first list of 23 candidates for the April 23 Assembly polls, renominating party chief Edappadi K Palaniswami from his home constituency of Edappadi.

But, Tehran may not accept such conditions given its reluctance to adhere to previous peace deals.

Trump's only option in the Iran war now is a nightmareTrump's pausing strikes against Iran for negotiations is being seen as the second betrayal by his voter base after a "peace president" waging war abroad. Trump shares Shehbaz Sharif's postUS President Donald Trump shared a X post from Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif, who said that Islamabad could host the US-Iran talks to end the Middle East war. In the post, Sharif said Pakistan "welcomes and fully supports" efforts to end the war.

sports.headline: Ben Duckett's withdrawal raises concerns over English players' future in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Ben Duckett's withdrawal from the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League has sparked fresh concerns over the reliability of English players in the league, with former India opener Aakash Chopra warning that franchises may rethink investing in young cricketers from England.

Dark chocolate for dark boy: L Sivaramakrishnan says he faced racism from teammates Laxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed how teenage encounters with racism and systemic bias shattered his confidence, offering a haunting new perspective on why one of India's greatest bowling prodigies saw his career unravel.

Livingstone still up for England selection despite explosive interview: Rob Key Rob Key said that Liam Livingstone is still under consideration for England selection despite his public outburst against the board and the current team management. Livingstone has been out of the England setup since 2025.

How can Rishabh Pant find consistency as a T20 player? Faf du Plessis decodes Faf du Plessis has said that Rishabh Pant needs to be a high-impact player to find consistency as a T20 player. The LSG captain has impressed in Tests for India but hasn't found the same vein of form in white-ball cricket.

Real Madrid injury mismanagement: Mbappe played with torn knee after wrong scan Real Madrid have come under intense scrutiny after reports of a serious medical error involving Kylian Mbappe, who was allegedly allowed to play with a knee injury following a diagnostic mix-up.

Can a reimagined Rajasthan Royals get it right under Riyan Parag? IPL 2026 preview: Rajasthan Royals head into the new season amid buzz of an ownership change and with Riyan Parag as their new captain. But the big question remains: can they finally end their long wait for glory?

Virat Kohli has set a great example: Kaif urges Rahane to learn from RCB star IPL 2026: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif advised KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane to take inspiration from Virat Kohli's improved game against spin and convert his starts into match-winning knocks.

Rajasthan Royals: The $67 million IPL shadow that became a $1.63 billion empire Starting as one of the low-key sides in IPL, Rajasthan Royals finds itself as an empire now after its USD 1.63 billion sale. From winning the inaugural IPL title under Shane Warne to being filled with controversies, here's a look at the Jaipur-based franchise.

Griezmann joins ex-Barcelona teammate Messi in MLS, signs for Orlando City French forward Antoine Griezmann is set to join MLS club Orlando City from Atletico Madrid in 2026. Griezmann will reunite with former Barcelona teammate and Inter Miami superstar Lionel Messi in the league.

IPL Impact Player rule to stay, says Sourav Ganguly after Axar Patel's criticism IPL 2026: All-rounders have openly expressed their disdain for it, but former India captain Sourav Ganguly on Tuesday said the Indian Premier League's controversial Impact Player rule is here to stay.

Most of ECB hierarchy should be sacked: Boycott slams England's Ashes reviewFormer England captain and legendary batter Geoffrey Boycott slammed ECB chief executive Richard Gould and managing director Rob Key over the review into England's Ashes defeat earlier in the summer.

A champion at last, Virat Kohli now walks into IPL 2026 without chainsIPL 2026: The wait is over, and so is the weight that followed Virat Kohli through every IPL season. Now, with nothing left to prove and only milestones ahead, IPL 2026 could unleash his most dangerous, unrestrained version yet.

Give us 0.5%: Netherlands cricketer's blunt take on Rs 16,660 crore RCB saleAfter RCB's Rs. 16,660 crore sale to a multi-industry consortium, Netherlands pacer Paul van Meekeren said that 0.5 percent of that price is enough to change Dutch cricket and help them bridge the gap to big teams in international cricket.

Salah to leave Liverpool at the end of the season, ends 9-year stint at AnfieldMohamed Salah will leave Liverpool at the end of the season, the club confirmed on Tuesday, bringing the curtain down on one of the most decorated spells in the club's history.

IPL champions RCB sold to Aditya Birla Group-led consortium for Rs 16,660 croreIPL franchise, Royal Challengers Bengaluru, has been sold to a consortium, led by Aditya Birla Group, for a record sum of over Rs 16,000 crore, marking the league's most expensive franchise takeover ever.

Andre Russell emotional after KKR retire his No. 12 jersey in special ceremonyIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders on Tuesday retired Andre Russell's No. 12 jersey in honour of his contributions to the team. Russell, who retired from IPL last year, joined the backroom staff for the upcoming season, starting March 28.

When you think of KKR, it's Shah Rukh Khan first and then Rinku Singh: Ajinkya RahaneIPL 2026: Rinku Singh named KKR vice-captain as Rahane and Nayar praised his growth, leadership and impact, with the T20 World Cup winner now taking on a bigger role.

Chess great Koneru Humpy slammed for pulling out of Candidates over security fearsCyprus Chess Federation chief Criton Tornaritis has criticised Koneru Humpy's withdrawal from the Women's Candidates over safety concerns, calling it a bad move that harms the sport and organisers.

KKR announce new vice-captain, Rinku Singh to be Ajinkya Rahane's deputyIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders on Tuesday announced Rinku Singh as their vice-captain for the new season. Ajinkya Rahane will continue to lead the three-time champions, who finished eighth in the 10-team league last season.

RCB vs SRH tickets sell out in a minute, fans allege lack of transparencyTickets for the IPL 2026 opener between RCB and SRH sold out within minutes, leaving fans frustrated, with many alleging lack of transparency and irregularities in the booking process.

Ben Duckett pulls out of IPL 2026, apologises to Delhi Capitals, faces 2-year banIPL 2026: Ben Duckett withdraws from IPL 2026 to focus on England duty, apologises to Delhi Capitals, and could face a ban under league rules for pulling out after being picked at the auction.

IPL as big as EPL and NBA, says Sourav Ganguly after Rajasthan Royals' takeover buzzIPL 2026: Sourav Ganguly says the IPL now rivals global giants like the EPL and NBA, as soaring valuations and rising global investment underline its rapid growth and commercial dominance.

After quitting PSL, Australia pacer joins CSK as Nathan Ellis's replacementIPL 2026: Australia's Spencer Johnson became the third player to quit Pakistan Super League and join the IPL as a replacement player. Chennai Super Kings signed the left-arm fast bowler for Rs 1.5 crore.

Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu to lead India's Thomas and Uber Cup campaigns in MayWhile Lakshya Sen will lead India's campaign in Thomas Cup, two-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu will return to action to lead the Uber Cup team in Horsens, Denmark, from April 24 to May 3.

Bengaluru stampede: What has RCB done to make Chinnaswamy safer for IPL 2026?IPL 2026: RCB have invested Rs 7 crore to overhaul crowd safety at Chinnaswamy Stadium, nearly a year after 11 fans died in a stampede during the team's trophy celebrations.

Rajasthan Royals sold for Rs 15,000 crore to US-based entrepreneur: ReportA US-led consortium has reportedly acquired Rajasthan Royals for USD 1.63 billion, setting a new benchmark in the Indian Premier League. Meanwhile, bidding for RCB intensifies, with valuations expected to cross USD 2 billion following their historic 2025 title and massive global demand.

Klopp rubbishes Real links, leaves door open for return to football managementFormer Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has strongly dismissed repeated speculation linking him with the managerial position at Real Madrid, labelling the reports as complete "nonsense" invented by "idiots".

BCCI bans match-day practice, announces stricter rules in updated guidelinesIPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has expanded its operational guidelines for this season, introducing stricter regulations, including a complete ban on match-day practice sessions, according to a report by Cricbuzz.

Smriti Mandhana retains 2nd spot, Harmanpreet rises to 14th in ICC T20I rankingsIndia batter Smriti Mandhana retained her second spot, while skipper Harmanpreet Kaur moved up to 14th in the latest ICC women's T20I rankings for batters on Tuesday.

RCB to keep 11 seats reserved in all matches as tribute to stampede victimsFollowing a tragic stampede and the loss of hosting rights, RCB returns to M Chinnaswamy Stadium with a permanent 11-seat tribute and AI-driven safety protocols for the 2026 IPL season.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
